title: After buying Platinum are you keeping your older DVDs
display_title: After buying Platinum are you keeping your older DVDs
wikitext:
[]: After buying Platinum, are you keeping your older DVDs?             Quote Post by Otaprince » Sun Nov 28, 2004 4:21 pm i'm unsure whether to keep or sell my old box set of Eva DVDs now that i'm buying the platinums. i'd like to keep the last few volumes, at least, for the non-director's cuts, and i'm not sure i'd get much on ebay anyway, but... who is doing what?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sun Nov 28, 2004 4:25 pm I wouldn't part with my Perfect Collection for any price.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Nov 28, 2004 5:48 pm I will keep mine, like you said once all of the platinums are out the perfect collection won't be worth much. Plus I will just turn around the perfect collection box so I can have those Eva eyes stare at me. It's freaky yet comforting.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2004 5:57 pm I'm considering trying to get a dirt cheap Perfect Collection for comparitive purposes. Especially as considering that the subtitles are different. Plus it might be helpful to have a second copy I can lend out.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Nov 28, 2004 6:52 pm Hopefully we will be able to do a trade up.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Sun Nov 28, 2004 7:48 pm I sold my Perfect Collection so I could get the Platinum Edition. :P Bought it for $135 at AnimeNation, and sold it for $20 to my best friend. Ugh... now that I think about it, that was a shit trade. Oh well... I can still borrow them from her if I want.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Sun Nov 28, 2004 9:28 pm I own the boxed set of the first released Eva DVDs. Will I part with them for the new platinums? Probably not. To be honest, I'm not all impressed with the audio in the platinum editions. During episode 2, I noticed that Eva 01 didn't make as much noise as it did in the original, there seemed to be sounds missing. Certain audio was revamped, but other pieces were left out, or unpolished. I really only want the platinums for their cast audio commentary. I love audio commentary. It makes me smile in ways the original episodes could never match ^^;;

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2004 9:52 pm You're a dubbie aren't you Kiel?

[]:             Quote Post by Beatle » Sun Nov 28, 2004 10:54 pm How about for those of us that have only the platinums? Is the Perfect worth getting as well?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2004 10:59 pm I would say no, as the quality for Platinum is much better. Some of the dub fans might dislike some of things they did to the audio. That's one reason why you might want the Perfect Collection as well. I doubt it though.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Nov 29, 2004 2:15 am Beside the picture jittering ( moving up and down ) after ep 8 , it is fine. So will probably keep mine.

[]:             Quote Post by Otaprince » Mon Nov 29, 2004 3:16 am does anyone know if that jitter was on the Japanese Eva DVDs? -- the originals, i mean, before Renewal of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Mon Nov 29, 2004 10:14 am In regard to what Evamonkey said, Yes, I am a dub fan, but that's not to say I don't love the Japanese with subtitles more than the other. I love them both, probably because Eva is one of the few english dubs I really enjoyed. ahhh...Amanda Wynn Lee, Tiffany Grant and Allison Keys...you light up my life . I'm still keeping my oldie but goodie DVDs tho

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 29, 2004 11:07 am Otaprince wrote: does anyone know if that jitter was on the Japanese Eva DVDs? -- the originals, i mean, before Renewal of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Mon Nov 29, 2004 2:30 pm If anyone is selling there old for there new ill take....well buy dont care if the cases are all messed up as long as the discs are okay.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Mon Nov 29, 2004 5:42 pm Kiel wrote: In regard to what Evamonkey said, Yes, I am a dub fan, but that's not to say I don't love the Japanese with subtitles more than the other. I love them both, probably because Eva is one of the few english dubs I really enjoyed. ahhh...Amanda Wynn Lee, Tiffany Grant and Allison Keys...you light up my life . I'm still keeping my oldie but goodie DVDs tho

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Nov 29, 2004 8:59 pm Ayanami wrote: I sold my Perfect Collection so I could get the Platinum Edition. :P Bought it for $135 at AnimeNation, and sold it for $20 to my best friend. Ugh... now that I think about it, that was a shit trade. Oh well... I can still borrow them from her if I want.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Mon Nov 29, 2004 9:56 pm Timesplitter 01 wrote: Ayanami wrote: I sold my Perfect Collection so I could get the Platinum Edition. :P Bought it for $135 at AnimeNation, and sold it for $20 to my best friend. Ugh... now that I think about it, that was a shit trade. Oh well... I can still borrow them from her if I want.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 29, 2004 10:05 pm One advantage the Perfect Collection has over the Platinum Edition is languages. The Perfect Collection has English, Japanese, French, and Spanish. Platinum only has English and Japanese. Strangely enough, some English speakers take a liking to the French and Spanish dubs.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Nov 29, 2004 10:33 pm But $20 for Eva ( all 8 dics). Thats ridiculous. It makes my blood boil. But really , you could have sell it to store for more , well about $20 more
summary:
[]: After buying Platinum, are you keeping your older DVDs?             Quote Post by Otaprince » Sun Nov 28, 2004 4:21 pm i'm unsure whether to keep or sell my old box set of Eva DVDs now that i'm buying the platinums. i'd like to keep the last few volumes, at least, for the non-director's cuts, and i'm not sure i'd get much on ebay anyway, but... who is doing what?

[]:         &n
